title: thymus hirtus subsp. serpylloides
intro: Thymus hirtus subsp. serpylloides, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues. The health benefits of Thymus hirtus subsp. serpylloides include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve digestion, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Thymus hirtus subsp. serpylloides can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine, as well as in aromatherapy and culinary applications.
